Category ceiling
7.0 HIP
This is what the HIP methodology rewards when a product in this category fully evidences material provenance, supply-chain integrity, repairability, and end-of-life pathways. It is the benchmark for verified assessment — not a product currently on sale.
✓ Verified Rating baby travel systems Assessed 2026-04-26 1.3

Pushchair — Category Ceiling

Category Ceiling

HIP Score
7.0/10
Standard
At category baseline (7.0)
Regenerative Index
-2.0
Extractive
At category average (-2.0)
HIP Mark Standard This verified product achieved a HIP Score of 7.0, qualifying for the HIP Mark Standard tier.

Score Breakdown

Material Scarcity Index
20% weight
8.5
The product relies on abundant materials (aluminium, steel, cotton, rubber, polypropylene) with no EU Critical Raw Materials present. It achieves a high score due to 58% recycled content and diverse supply chains across the EU, India, and Sri Lanka, avoiding single-country concentration risks.
Supply Chain Risk
18% weight
6.0
The pushchair's supply chain is geographically diversified across four stable jurisdictions (EU, UK, India, Sri Lanka) with an OECD due-diligence framework in place and a verified alternative supplier. However, the lack of third-party audit verification for the specific material origins (likely steel, plastics, and aluminum) and the absence of explicit confirmation that no EU Critical Raw Materials are used limits the score to the moderate risk range.
Recyclability & Circularity
18% weight
7.5
The pushchair features a modular design with tool-free disassembly and a verified take-back scheme, enabling high material recovery. With 78% of the product weight entering practical recycling streams and compliant labelling, it meets the criteria for a well-designed product for recovery, falling just short of the 80% threshold required for the highest circularity tier.
Repairability
13% weight
8.0
The pushchair features tool-free clips and a 10-year spare parts commitment with a public service manual, meeting the criteria for 'Designed for Repair'. Although it is not within EU Ecodesign scope, the explicit 10-year parts guarantee and documentation place it in the 7-8 range, approaching exemplary status.
Social & Environmental Impact
8% weight
8.0
The product scores highly due to manufacturing in the UK, an OECD jurisdiction with strong labour enforcement, combined with a completed RBA audit and multiple environmental certifications including ISO 14001, GOTS, and ASI. While the manufacturer claims ILO compliance, the presence of the verified RBA audit and robust environmental standards justifies a score in the 'Verified Good Practice' range.
Product Longevity
8% weight
8.0
The product achieves an Above Average Lifespan score due to a 5-year warranty and a stated design life of 10 years, which aligns with the premium appliance benchmark. The inclusion of a modular design further supports long-term repairability and extends the product's usable life beyond standard mass-market norms.
Regenerative Index
15% weight · scale −10 to +10
-2.0
The product scores in the Extractive band (-5 to -1) due to 58% recycled content combined with a verified take-back and closed-loop programme. Not Depleting because recycled content is >= 50% with a verified take-back scheme, which elevates the score above the -7 floor. While FSC-certified rubber and GOTS organic cotton are present, the absence of Regenerative Organic Certified (ROC) inputs prevents a shift to the Restorative or Regenerative bands.

MSI 20% · SCR 18% · RC 18% · R 13% · SEI 8% · PL 8% · RI 15% = 100%

About the HIP Methodology ▸
Generic ratings are intentionally conservative. They represent only what the HIP methodology can confirm from publicly available specification data, before any manufacturer-supplied evidence is reviewed. Most categories sit in the 2–4 range at the pre-verification baseline. This is the methodology working as designed: it makes the gap between current market practice and demonstrable resilience legible to consumers, manufacturers, and procurement buyers. Verified assessment is the path across that gap, and the HIP Mark is the proof of crossing it. Read the full methodology →

Consumer Summary

This pushchair earns a HIP Score of 7.0 out of 10, indicating a solid performance in durability and repairability with a 10-year design life and easy disassembly. Its Regenerative Index is -2.0, reflecting a strong start with 58% recycled content and a verified take-back scheme that supports material recovery. Overall, it is a well-built travel system designed to last and return materials to the cycle effectively.

What This Means For You

When buying a baby travel system, look for products with long warranties and clear instructions for repair or disassembly to ensure they last through multiple children. Prioritize items that use recycled materials and offer a take-back program, as these features help close the loop on waste. Avoid pushchairs that are difficult to fix or lack a plan for what happens to the product at the end of its life.

Data Sources

This rating draws on the following public sources:
USGS Mineral Resources Program (public domain) · EU Critical Raw Materials List 2023 (CC BY 4.0) · World Bank Worldwide Governance Indicators (CC BY 4.0) · LLM training knowledge (for assumed fields — no manufacturer submission)
All sources are public domain or published under open data licences. Full source list →

About this rating: HIP scores are based on publicly available data and manufacturer-submitted information at the assessment date. They are estimates of material resilience — not guarantees of product performance, safety, or future supply conditions. Resourcehip accepts no liability for decisions made in reliance on these ratings.

Scope: This is a verified product rating for Category Ceiling, assessed using manufacturer-submitted data and public category data. Produced under Resourcehip Scoring Methodology 1.3.

AI disclosure: This rating was produced using an AI scoring pipeline (local, no cloud — no data shared externally) and reviewed and approved by a human assessor before publication. Scoring model: qwen3.5:35b. The model's training data influences the findings — see the methodology for caveats. AI transparency notice →

Not financial or investment advice. Ratings are for consumer information only. Dispute this rating · Our methodology · All ratings · Submit updated data
